title: Pongelion fauvelianum

Pongamia pinnata, also known as Pongelion fauvelianum, is a tree species native to the Indian subcontinent and Southeast Asia. The health benefits of Pongamia pinnata include its potential anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties, which may help to manage various health conditions. The bioactive constituents of Pongamia pinnata include isoflavones, terpenoids, and fatty acids, among others. In traditional medicine, Pongamia pinnata is commonly used to prepare herbal preparations such as oils, extracts, and infusions, which are used to treat a range of health issues, including skin conditions and digestive problems.
constituents: Pongelion fauvelianum contains a range of bioactive compounds including flavonoids, saponins, and phenolic acids. Additionally, the phenolic acids found in Pongelion fauvelianum may have potential in the prevention and treatment of various diseases.
parts: Pongelion fauvelianum is a species that has been studied for its potential medicinal properties. The roots and leaves of this plant have been traditionally used in folk medicine.
